title: How hinted handoff preserves write availability
description: When a replica is down, the coordinator stores a hint and replays it later, which keeps writes available but is not a consistency guarantee.
body: Reference for understanding write availability under node failure. If a replica is unreachable during a write, the coordinator can store a hint and replay it once the node returns, so writes stay available even with a replica down (within the hint window). Use when reasoning about availability versus durability. Do not treat hinted handoff as a substitute for repair or for an adequate consistency level; hints expire after a window and a coordinator failure loses them. Validation signals include hints metrics showing replay after a node restart and repair still reconciling any gaps. Risk boundary: relying on hints alone for consistency risks lost updates if the window lapses, so pair an appropriate write CL with scheduled repair rather than leaning on handoff.
